      <title>Cooper Flagg Beats Out Kon Knueppel To Win 2025-26 NBA Rookie Of The Year</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285332/Cooper-Flagg-Beats-Out-Kon-Knueppel-To-Win-2025-26-NBA-Rookie-Of-The-Year</link>
      <description><p>Cooper Flagg has been named the <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/nba/awards/by-type/Rookie-of-the-Year/5">2025-26 NBA Rookie of the Year</a>, beating out former Duke teammate Kon Knueppel. Flagg received 56 first place votes compared to 44 for Knueppel  </p>
<p>Flagg averaged 21.0 points, 6.7 rebounds, 4.5 assists and 1.2 steals in 33.5 minutes over 70 games for the Dallas Mavericks. Flagg was the No. 1 overall pick in the 2025 NBA draft out of Duke. </p>
<p>"This is a truly great honor," said Flagg. "I'm grateful to receive this award and thankful to everyone in the Dallas Mavericks organization who believed in me from day one. None of this happens without my teammates, coaches and the people around me pushing me every day. I came here to compete and help this team win. This is just one step forward in what we're building." </p>
<p>Knueppel appeared in 81 games for the Charlotte Hornets, averaging 18.5 points, 5.3 rebounds and 3.4 assists. Knueppel shot 42.5 percent on 642 three-point attempts, making a rookie record 273 three-pointers.</p>
<p>VJ Edgecombe finished third in Rookie of the Year voting.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Mon, 27 Apr 2026 19:18:48 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285332/Cooper-Flagg-Beats-Out-Kon-Knueppel-To-Win-2025-26-NBA-Rookie-Of-The-Year</guid>

   <item>
      <title>18 Team Draft Lottery Reform Considered Frontrunner</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285315/18-Team-Draft-Lottery-Reform-Considered-Frontrunner</link>
      <description><p>The NBA is hosting a virtual meeting with front office executives on Thursday. All 30 teams will be attendance as the league continues to discuss Draft reform, starting with how the lottery works. The league's Option 1 proposal is considered the frontrunner at this point.</p>
<p>Option 1 would see the lottery consist of 18 teams: the 14 teams who didn't make the playoffs, plus the four teams that made the playoffs through the Play-In Tournament. The bottom 10 teams (those didn't make the postseason at all) would split 80% of the lottery odds even 8% odd apiece to win the lottery. The remaining 20% would be divided between the other eight teams.</p>
<p>The NBA is taking a collaborative approach with their partners to curb tanking. The Board of Governors will have final say on any changes, but the league has involved front office executives and the NBPA in discussion. The Board of Governors is expected to vote on changes next month. Any changes would be implemented with the 2026-27 season and would impact the 2027 NBA Draft.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Mon, 27 Apr 2026 09:36:27 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285315/18-Team-Draft-Lottery-Reform-Considered-Frontrunner</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Silver_Adam_nba_220225.jpg" />
   </item>

      <title>NBA Expansion Bidding More Competitive In Las Vegas Than Seattle</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285333/NBA-Expansion-Bidding-More-Competitive-In-Las-Vegas-Than-Seattle</link>
      <description><p>Multiple investor groups have expressed early interest in NBA expansion franchises in Las Vegas and Seattle, with the league targeting fees as high as $10 billion per team. Las Vegas is drawing stronger initial interest than Seattle, according to sources familiar with the discussions.</p>
<p>No formal bids have been submitted. The league's financial adviser, PJT Partners, is screening potential ownership groups ahead of a structured bidding process expected later this year. PJT and the NBA declined to comment.</p>
<p>League executives believe each franchise could fetch between $7 billion and $10 billion. The Los Angeles Lakers sold for $10 billion last year, the only majority-stake sports transaction globally to reach that figure. The Boston Celtics set a prior benchmark in 2025 at $6.1 billion.</p>
<p>"The only discussion in the room was understanding the math around dilution, in terms of projection, and what's the direct reduction in existing revenues to teams if we were to expand beginning in 2028 or 2029," Commissioner Adam Silver said in March.</p>
<p>Among those pursuing a Las Vegas franchise are NBA Hall of Famer Magic Johnson, Vegas Golden Knights owner Bill Foley, and Avenue Capital Group founder Marc Lasry. Johnson met with Nevada Governor Joe Lombardo earlier this year regarding a potential bid.</p>
<p>Foley, who co-controls T-Mobile Arena with Anschutz Entertainment Group and MGM Resorts International, confirmed his interest to a Nevada television station in March.</p>
<p>On the Seattle side, Samantha Holloway, through her firm One Roof Sports and Entertainment, which oversees the NHL's Kraken ownership group, is preparing a bid. Seattle has been without an NBA franchise since the SuperSonics relocated to Oklahoma City in 2008.</p>
<p>"We are ready to work with the Commissioner and the league, at their direction, to explore bringing the NBA back to Seattle," a One Roof spokesperson said.</p>
<p>The NBA's board of governors is expected to vote later this year on approving one or both franchises. The league's last expansion came in 2004, when Charlotte paid $300 million for the Bobcats.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Mon, 27 Apr 2026 22:20:12 EDT</pubDate>

      <title>Pat Riley Vows To Stay With Heat, Demands Roster Overhaul Around Bam Adebayo</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285329/Pat-Riley-Vows-To-Stay-With-Heat-Demands-Roster-Overhaul-Around-Bam-Adebayo</link>
      <description><p>Miami Heat president Pat Riley declared Monday he has no plans to leave the organization while expressing sharp frustration over the franchise's prolonged struggles, speaking at his annual end-of-season press conference.</p>
<p>"I'm not going to retire. I'm not going to resign. I'm not going to step aside," Riley said. "I want another parade down Biscayne Boulevard."</p>
<p>Miami finished 43-39, tenth in the Eastern Conference, marking the club's fourth straight Play-In Tournament appearance. <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/nba/team-stats/2025/Advanced_Stats/Team_Totals/Regular_Season/ediff/desc">The Heat ranked 12th in offensive efficiency and 14th in defense</a>, their worst defensive standing relative to the league since 2014-15.</p>
<p>Riley singled out Bam Adebayo, who turns 29 in July, as the foundation of any rebuild. Adebayo averaged 20 points and 10 rebounds for the first time in his career but shot a career-low 44.2 percent from the field while expanding his perimeter game. Riley called efficiency his primary concern going forward.</p>
<p>"He deserves to win more," Riley said of Adebayo, ruling out any possibility of trading him.</p>
<p>The Heat also navigated a difficult season that included Tyler Herro appearing in just 33 games, Nikola Jovic shooting a career-low 36.6 percent, and the waiving of Terry Rozier following a federal sports gambling indictment.</p>
<p>Bright spots included Norman Powell's first All-Star selection and Bam Adebayo's 83-point performance against Washington on March 10, the second-highest single-game scoring total in NBA history.</p>
<p>"We're not good enough, and we're not happy with it," Riley said. "I think this is the first time in three years we can do something about it."</p></description>
      <pubDate>Mon, 27 Apr 2026 15:19:04 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285329/Pat-Riley-Vows-To-Stay-With-Heat-Demands-Roster-Overhaul-Around-Bam-Adebayo</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Riley_Pat_mia_241227.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Terry Rozier Faces Superseding Indictment As Prosecutors Allege He Solicited, Accepted Bribe</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285334/Terry-Rozier-Faces-Superseding-Indictment-As-Prosecutors-Allege-He-Solicited-Accepted-Bribe</link>
      <description><p>Federal prosecutors plan to file a superseding indictment against Terry Rozier, adding bribery allegations to an existing sports gambling case. A lawyer from the U.S. Attorney's Office for the Eastern District of New York announced the new charges Monday during a federal court hearing.</p>
<p>The disclosure came as Rozier's attorney, Jim Trusty, was seeking dismissal of the original case. Trusty told the court prosecutors had informed him of the new charges just two hours before the hearing. Rozier has pleaded not guilty to the initial counts.</p>
<p>"We have developed evidence that Mr. Rozier solicited and accepted a bribe," said Eastern District prosecutor Kaitlin Ferrell during Monday's hearing.</p>
<p>Prosecutors will present the charges to a grand jury, alleging sports bribery and honest services fraud. They contend Rozier deprived "the NBA and the Charlotte Hornets of Mr. Rozier's honest services." The superseding indictment is expected by mid-May.</p>
<p>Rozier currently faces two federal wire fraud charges stemming from an October arrest. Prosecutors allege he tipped off associate Deniro Laster about plans to exit a March 2023 game early while playing for Charlotte. Laster allegedly sold that information to gamblers who wagered on Rozier's playing time.</p>
<p>The Miami Heat waived Rozier earlier this month. The NBA had suspended him following the original charges, though an arbitrator ruled he must still receive his $26.4 million salary. A status hearing is scheduled for June 10.</p>
<p>Rozier is one of six defendants in a broad federal case alleging non-public NBA player information was sold to sports gamblers. A seventh defendant, former NBA player Damon Jones, is set to change his plea to guilty Tuesday, becoming the first defendant in the case to do so. Jones will also plead guilty in a separate case involving an alleged rigged poker ring, which includes former NBA player Chauncey Billups, who has pleaded not guilty.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Mon, 27 Apr 2026 22:32:46 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285334/Terry-Rozier-Faces-Superseding-Indictment-As-Prosecutors-Allege-He-Solicited-Accepted-Bribe</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Rozier_Terry_mia_240226.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Kevin Durant Not Ruled Out Of Returning In Lakers' Series</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285321/Kevin-Durant-Not-Ruled-Out-Of-Returning-In-Lakers-Series</link>
      <description><p>Kevin Durant remains sidelined with an ankle bone bruise, but Houston Rockets coach Ime Udoka left the door open for the veteran forward's return after Sunday's 115-96 Game 4 victory over the Los Angeles Lakers extended the first-round playoff series.</p>
<p>Durant has now missed three of four games this postseason. He sat out the series opener with a bruised right knee, returned for Game 2, scoring 23 points in 41 minutes before re-injuring his ankle late, then missed Games 3 and 4.</p>
<p>"Yeah, a possibility, for sure," Udoka said. "I didn't know how bad it was initially and then we got that prognosis. But he's doing what he can to get swelling out and mobility back. And just like the knee, we weren't sure when he was going to come back, but he snapped back pretty quickly to be available for Game 2. So it is a true game-to-game, day-to-day thing."</p>
<p>Udoka described the bone bruise as painful and restricting Durant's movement, while stopping short of ruling him out for the remainder of the playoffs. Game 5 tips off Wednesday night in Los Angeles.</p>
<p>Houston dropped Game 3 Friday without Durant, surrendering a six-point lead in the final 30 seconds of regulation before falling 112-108 in overtime. The Rockets responded Sunday behind strong contributions from healthy roster members to keep their postseason run alive.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Mon, 27 Apr 2026 11:13:58 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285321/Kevin-Durant-Not-Ruled-Out-Of-Returning-In-Lakers-Series</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Durant_Kevin_hou_251010.jpg" />
   </item>

      <title>Paige Bueckers Calls Relationship With Azzi Fudd Private, Pledges Professionalism On Court</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285331/Paige-Bueckers-Calls-Relationship-With-Azzi-Fudd-Private-Pledges-Professionalism-On-Court</link>
      <description><p>Dallas Wings star Paige Bueckers used her opening statement at Monday's media day to directly address public interest in her relationship with teammate Azzi Fudd, declaring the matter private while pledging full professionalism on the court.</p>
<p>"Quite frankly, I believe me and Azzi's personal relationship is nobody's business but our own, and what we choose to share is completely up to us," said Bueckers, the reigning WNBA Rookie of the Year and the No. 1 overall pick in the 2025 draft.</p>
<p>Bueckers made clear she intends to address the topic only once. Future questions, she said, will be met with deflection or a reference back to Monday's statement.</p>
<p>"Me and Azzi have always been the utmost professional," Bueckers said. "We've never let anything that happens off the court carry onto the court. That's what we continue to do."</p>
<p>Both were top-ranked high school recruits who played together with USA Basketball before becoming UConn teammates for four years, winning a national championship together in 2025.</p>
<p>Fudd was selected No. 1 in this month's WNBA draft after a standout senior season at UConn, averaging 17.3 points and shooting 45 percent from three. Wings general manager Curt Miller said the evaluation process "always came back to Azzi" regardless of her connection to Bueckers.</p>
<p>"Azzi Fudd was a No. 1 draft pick because she earned it, and it had nothing to do with me," Bueckers said.</p>
<p>Dallas opens its season May 9 against the Indiana Fever in a matchup featuring the last four No. 1 overall picks.</p></description>

      <title>Five-Star Finnish Forward Miikka Muurinen Commits To Arkansas Vaulting To No. 1 Recruiting Class</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285328/Five-Star-Finnish-Forward-Miikka-Muurinen-Commits-To-Arkansas-Vaulting-To-No-1-Recruiting-Class</link>
      <description><p>Finland native Miikka Muurinen, a five-star forward with a 95 scouting grade, has committed to Arkansas, giving John Calipari the No. 1 recruiting class in the country, the 7-footer told ESPN Monday.</p>
<p>Muurinen spent time at several American high schools before signing with Partizan Belgrade in Serbia, where he appeared in 14 games this season including six in EuroLeague play. He took an official visit to Arkansas in fall 2024 and maintained close contact with the program in the 18 months since.</p>
<p>"Since my visit a year and a half ago, they've been consistent and committed to recruiting me," Muurinen told ESPN. "When I left my Arkansas visit, I was not only excited about the program but also about the people, and I said to myself, those are the type of people I want to be around on a daily basis."</p>
<p>"The thing about Coach Calipari that stood out was that he kept it real with me," Muurinen said. "He has had so much success with so many one-and-done players, and I really like his coaching style both on and off the court."</p>
<p>Muurinen joins a loaded 2026 class that includes top-ranked guard Jordan Smith Jr., five-star forward JaShawn Andrews, and top-30 wing Abdou Toure. The addition gives Arkansas four likely five-star prospects and a projected preseason top-10 roster.</p>
<p>At the Nike Hoop Summit earlier this month, Muurinen posted 10 points and eight rebounds in 15 minutes for the World Team. He also represented Finland's senior national team at FIBA EuroBasket last summer, averaging 6.6 points in eight games.</p>
<p>"I'm going to Arkansas to help them win a national championship," Muurinen said, "and to play with a great roster of guys."</p></description>

   <item>
      <title>Jayson Tatum's Achilles Recovery Aided By Surgical Advances Honed On Ballerinas</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285322/Jayson-Tatums-Achilles-Recovery-Aided-By-Surgical-Advances-Honed-On-Ballerinas</link>
      <description><p>Jayson Tatum's rapid recovery from a torn Achilles was aided by his proximity to one of the world's best surgeons who had performed the procedure for years on elite ballerinas. </p>
<p>Tatum ruptured his Achilles during a playoff game at Madison Square Garden last May. Within 16 hours, he was on the operating table at the Hospital for Special Surgery in Manhattan, under the care of surgeon Martin O'Malley. Before treating NBA stars, O'Malley learned his craft from ballerinas.</p>
<p>Long before repairing the Achilles tendons of players such as Kevin Durant, the first All-NBA player in recent history to return to peak form after rupturing the ligament, O'Malley trained under the primary surgeon for the American Ballet Theatre. His earliest prominent patients were New York's top ballerinas. Operating on those performers taught him a technique that leaves the repaired tendon slightly tighter than the healthy one, preventing rehabilitation from stretching it loose.</p>
<p>"Those are not easy ones," O'Malley said. "To get them where they can walk on their toes for six months."</p>
<p>After years studying the 10-inch tendon that can end athletic careers, O'Malley arrived at the guiding principle that the sooner a patient reaches the operating table, the stronger their chances of full recovery.</p>
<p>O'Malley's clinic is located less than two miles from Madison Square Garden, allowing for an unusually rapid surgical response following Tatum's injury.</p>
<p>"As soon as the Achilles ruptures, things are going the wrong way," O'Malley said. "You get atrophy, change in physiology from the minute you rupture it. The best thing to do is to fix it faster."</p>
<p>The Celtics, anticipating a full season lost, restructured significant portions of their roster following Tatum's injury. Instead, he returned in March and has since played at an All-Star level.</p>
<p>"I had a motivated, freak athlete," O'Malley said. "So that really helped me a lot."</p>
<p>"A lot of things had to go right," Tatum said, "to even have the opportunity to come back and play."</p></description>
      <pubDate>Mon, 27 Apr 2026 11:53:40 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285322/Jayson-Tatums-Achilles-Recovery-Aided-By-Surgical-Advances-Honed-On-Ballerinas</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Tatum_Jayson_250502.jpg" />
   </item>

   <item>
      <title>Alperen Sengun Pregame Speech Fuels Rockets' Game 4 Rout Of Lakers</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285319/Alperen-Sengun-Pregame-Speech-Fuels-Rockets-Game-4-Rout-Of-Lakers</link>
      <description><p>Alperen Sengun addressed his Houston Rockets teammates after morning shootaround as they faced elimination, then backed his words with a 19-point performance in a commanding 115-96 victory over the Los Angeles Lakers in Game 4.</p>
<p>The win prevents a sweep and returns the Western Conference first-round series to Los Angeles for Game 5 on Wednesday. <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/betting/basketball-picks/nba/">The Lakers are 2.5 point favorites over the Rockets with the series returning to Crypto.com Arena</a>.</p>
<p>Sengun gathered the group after acknowledging the team's prolonged absence of key personnel. Amen Thompson, who led all scorers with 23 points on 10-of-16 shooting, credited the pregame address with restoring the team's belief following a painful overtime defeat in Game 3.</p>
<p>"He was just letting us know he didn't want to go home," Thompson said. "I was proud of him just taking that leadership role and just pulling us all back in there and being like, 'This is not how we end it. If we go out, we're going to go out and fight, but we're not thinking about going out.'"</p>
<p>"I think [Jae'Sean] Tate and me is the only guys left from the rebuild," Sengun said. "And I think this is my team, so I need to take care of everybody."</p>
<p>Houston's defense proved equally decisive. The Rockets forced 24 Lakers turnovers, generating 30 points, their highest single-game total from turnovers since a 2013 playoff contest against Oklahoma City, per ESPN Insights. Tari Eason recorded five steals, becoming the first Rocket to post 20 points and five steals while shooting above 70% in a playoff game since steals were officially tracked beginning in 1973-74.</p>
<p>"Backs against the wall, coming to perform," Thompson said. "Today we were making shots and capitalizing on turnovers."</p>
<p><a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/nba/stats/2026/Advanced_Stats/Qualified/points/All/desc/1/Playoffs">Sengun ranks eighth in the NBA Playoffs in PER with a mark of 23.5</a>.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Mon, 27 Apr 2026 11:07:28 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285319/Alperen-Sengun-Pregame-Speech-Fuels-Rockets-Game-4-Rout-Of-Lakers</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Sengun_Alperen_hou_211109.jpg" />
   </item>

   <item>
      <title>Joel Embiid On Returning To Sixers' Lineup In Game 4 Loss: 'It's Tough'</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285314/Joel-Embiid-On-Returning-To-Sixers-Lineup-In-Game-4-Loss-Its-Tough</link>
      <description><p>Joel Embiid finished with 26 points and 10 rebounds in the Philadelphia 76ers' 128-96 loss to the Boston Celtics in Game 4 to go down 3-1 in the series.</p>
<p>It was Embiid's first game in almost three weeks after undergoing an emergency appendectomy.</p>
<p>"It's tough," said Embiid after the game. "It's tough. But you try to do the best job possible with the conditions. Still got to go out there and try to play well and win a basketball game and we didn't do that tonight. We didn't play well."</p>
<p>Embiid added that he experienced complications following the surgery, and that he had only scrimmaged once before Sunday.</p>
<p>Payton Pritchard lead the Celtics with 32 points. Pritchard also hit 11 3's across both games in Philadelphia.</p>
<p>"It's just more playing with that aggression, playing with that spark," said Pritchard.</p>
<p>Jayson Tatum, who finished with 30 points, said that the Celtics played with desperation.</p>
<p>"We want to be the more desperate team," said Tatum. "Nine years in the playoffs, it never goes how you think it's going to go. However long it takes, four, six, five, seven games, just be prepared to do whatever it takes for however long it takes. And that's just the kind of mindset that we try to have."</p>
<p>Embiid then looked forward to Tuesday's Game 5 in Boston.</p>
<p>"Down 3-1, it's been done before," Embiid said. "Getting one in Boston is going to be tough. That's a really good team. We know what they do well, we know some of the mistakes we made tonight, so now it's on us to try to figure out how to fix them."</p></description>
      <pubDate>Mon, 27 Apr 2026 02:33:06 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285314/Joel-Embiid-On-Returning-To-Sixers-Lineup-In-Game-4-Loss-Its-Tough</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Embiid_Joel_phi_260406.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Rockets Defeat Lakers 115-96 In Game 4 To Avoid Sweep</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285313/Rockets-Defeat-Lakers-115-96-In-Game-4-To-Avoid-Sweep</link>
      <description><p>The Houston Rockets beat the Los Angeles Lakers 115-96 in Game 4 to avoid a series sweep on Sunday.</p>
<p>Amen Thompson scored 23 points, with Tari Eason adding 20 in the win.</p>
<p>The rest of the Rockets' starting lineup all scored at least 16 points.</p>
<p>"I'm proud of the guys," Thompson said. "Backs against the wall, us coming to perform, but we know we can do that all the time. And I feel like today we were making shots. It's the first time we were really making shots and we were capitalizing on the turnovers."'</p>
<p>Deandre Ayton lead the Lakers with 19 points and 10 rebounds before getting ejected in the third quarter for hitting Alperen Sengun in the face.</p>
<p>LeBron James finished with 10 points and 9 assists on 2-of-9 shooting.</p>
<p>The Lakers also had 23 turnovers in the game.</p>
<p>"If we want to win this series, we have to protect the ball and we have to defensive rebound and we have to be able to obviously bring that toughness which we did tonight," James said. "I'm not worried about that. But the turnovers obviously killed us from start to finish."</p>
<p>Kevin Durant missed the game with a <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285302/Kevin-Durant-Likely-To-Miss-Game-4-Due-To-Bone-Bruise-In-Sprained-Left-Ankle">bone bruise in his sprained left ankle</a>.</p>
<p>Game 5 takes place on Wednesday in Los Angeles.</p></description>

   <item>
      <title>Billy Donovan Negotiated Out Clause In Bulls' Extension During 2025 Offseason</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285312/Billy-Donovan-Negotiated-Out-Clause-In-Bulls-Extension-During-2025-Offseason</link>
      <description><p>Billy Donovan quietly positioned himself for a clean return to the coaching market months before stepping away from the Chicago Bulls, negotiating a contract clause during the 2025 offseason that granted him a personal exit option for the 2026-27 season.</p>
<p>Donovan secured the extension after the Bulls blocked the New York Knicks from interviewing him as a potential replacement for Tom Thibodeau. The deal, however, contained an immediate out at Donovan's sole discretion, a provision sources say reflected a shared understanding between Donovan and Bulls staffers that a rebuild was the franchise's most likely direction.</p>
<p>The 60-year-old leveraged that organizational uncertainty to obtain terms that ultimately put him back on the open market entirely on his own terms.</p>
<p>Chicago had been willing to retain Donovan at virtually any cost, with expanded player personnel responsibilities among the incentives available to him. Despite that, Donovan chose to exercise his option and walk away, mirroring his departure from the Oklahoma City Thunder ahead of the Thunder's 2020 rebuild.</p>
<p>With Taylor Jenkins hired in Milwaukee and Michael Malone departing for North Carolina, Donovan now stands as the top available head coaching candidate in the league. League sources anticipate he will draw serious interest from prominent positions, including some that have not yet officially become available, depending on playoff outcomes.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Sun, 26 Apr 2026 23:10:02 EDT</pubDate>

      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Donovan_Billy_chi_240410.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Steve Kerr: Warriors Exit Would Be 'Easy Decision' If Steph Curry, Draymond Green Were Retiring</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285311/Steve-Kerr-Warriors-Exit-Would-Be-Easy-Decision-If-Steph-Curry-Draymond-Green-Were-Retiring</link>
      <description><p>Steve Kerr remains undecided if he will return as head coach of the Golden State Warriors next season and admitted in a lengthy and personal interview with the New Yorker that the decision is complicated by the fact that Stephen Curry and Draymond Green are continuing their careers.</p>
<p><a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/nba/teams/Golden-State-Warriors/9/Playoff-History">The Warriors last won a championship in 2022 and have won just two playoff series in two of the past four seasons</a> as they've missed the playoffs altogether in the other two seasons.</p>
<p>"This is a really interesting situation, and I'm very respectful of the organization and their place in the universe right now," Kerr said of the Warriors. "And I know how this stuff works. Most coaching runs just last a certain amount of time, and then it's best for everybody to move forward. And what we have to figure out is whether now is that time, because what complicates it is we still have Steph and Draymond."</p>
<p>Both Curry and Green have one season remaining on their contracts. Curry remains one of the NBA's best players when healthy and has interest in signing a contract extension with the Warriors.</p>
<p class="paywall">"And I don't want to abandon those guys," added Kerr. "If Steph and Draymond were retiring this year, I think this would be an easy decision: we all go out together and the organization takes their new path. But it's not that easy because I think Steph's going to play another couple of years and I think we can still do some good things together. But these are all conversations that will happen in the next week or two and we'll figure it out. And whatever happens, it's going to end well. I know that, because it's too important not to."</p>
<p class="paywall">The Warriors have been active in pursuing trades for the likes of Giannis Antetokounmpo and Kawhi Leonard, but it is debatable how prudent it would be for the front office to send multiple first round picks in a win-now trade considering how far away they are from truly competing for a title. </p></description>
      <pubDate>Sun, 26 Apr 2026 22:52:44 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285311/Steve-Kerr-Warriors-Exit-Would-Be-Easy-Decision-If-Steph-Curry-Draymond-Green-Were-Retiring</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Kerr_Steve_gsw_240113.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Spurs Overcome 19-Point Deficit Behind Victor Wembanyama's Dominant Return To Win Game 4</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285310/Spurs-Overcome-19-Point-Deficit-Behind-Victor-Wembanyamas-Dominant-Return-To-Win-Game-4</link>
      <description><p>Victor Wembanyama recorded 27 points, 11 rebounds, seven blocks and four steals Sunday in his return from a concussion, powering San Antonio past Portland 114-93 in Game 4 to claim a 3-1 series lead. The Spurs erased a 19-point deficit in the process.</p>
<p>The victory marked the first time in NBA history a team trailed by 15 or more points at halftime and won by 15 or more. San Antonio outscored Portland 73-35 in the second half, which is the second-largest second-half scoring differential in playoff history, per ESPN Research.</p>
<p>"It's special, obviously," Wembanyama said. "I had lots of emotions in me before the game, obviously, excitement, frustration. So, I let it all out today."</p>
<p>Wembanyama had been cleared through the NBA's concussion protocol but did not receive final league clearance until roughly one hour before Game 4 tipoff, preventing a Game 3 return the team had anticipated.</p>
<p>"The way the situation was handled was very disappointing," Wembanyama said. "Not on the Spurs. But as I say, I won't get into details."</p>
<p>De'Aaron Fox led all scorers with 28 points. Devin Vassell contributed nine points during a decisive third-quarter surge in which San Antonio opened on a 13-0 run, cutting a 17-point halftime deficit to four within four minutes.</p>
<p>Wembanyama is now the fourth player since 1974, joining Patrick Ewing, David Robinson and Alonzo Mourning, to post 60 points, 20 rebounds and 10 blocks across his first three career playoff games.</p>
<p>"That's Vic being the best player in the world," Vassell said. "You expect that type of greatness from him every game."</p>
<p>San Antonio hosts Game 5 on Tuesday at Frost Bank Center with <a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/betting/basketball-picks/nba/">the Spurs 11.5 point favorites to close out the series</a>.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Sun, 26 Apr 2026 22:06:29 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285310/Spurs-Overcome-19-Point-Deficit-Behind-Victor-Wembanyamas-Dominant-Return-To-Win-Game-4</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Wembanyama_Victor_sas_240516.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Raptors Beat Cavaliers 93-89 To Even Series 2-2 Despite Historic 3-Point Shooting Struggles</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285309/Raptors-Beat-Cavaliers-93-89-To-Even-Series-2-2-Despite-Historic-3-Point-Shooting-Struggles</link>
      <description><p>The Toronto Raptors defeated the Cleveland Cavaliers 93-89 in Game 4 Sunday, tying their first-round series at 2-2 despite shooting 4-for-30 from 3-point range, the lowest such percentage with at least 25 attempts in a playoff victory in NBA history.</p>
<p>Toronto shot just 32% from the field overall. Cleveland shot 37%. The teams combined to shoot 20% from deep, the fourth-lowest combined 3-point percentage in a playoff game since the 3-point era began.</p>
<p>"We just never flinched; we continued guarding and guarding," Raptors coach Darko Rajakovic said. "Whatever it takes, just find a way to win the game."</p>
<p>Scottie Barnes and Brandon Ingram each scored 23 points, outpacing Cleveland's star duo of Donovan Mitchell (20 points) and James Harden (19 points). Ingram converted three of Toronto's four 3-pointers, including the team's sole made triple in the second half.</p>
<p>"We all communicated with each other, just keep shooting," Ingram said. "We're getting good looks. Not only 3s, we missed some layups. We missed a lot of open looks."</p>
<p>Barnes was dominant on both ends as held Mitchell to a 1-for-8 shooting mark when serving as primary defender. Cleveland shot just 3-for-12 when Barnes contested a shot. The performance followed his 33-point effort in Game 3.</p>
<p>Harden struggled significantly, recording seven turnovers against only six made field goals. Mitchell and Harden combined for 11 turnovers.</p>
<p>"Guys got to be aggressive," Harden said. "It's time for us to make an adjustment in the sense of just being aggressive."</p>
<p><a href="https://basketball.realgm.com/betting/basketball-picks/nba/">Game 5 is scheduled for Wednesday in Cleveland with the Cavaliers 6.5 point favorites over the Raptors</a>.</p></description>

   </item>

      <title>Matt Lloyd, Bryson Graham, Dennis Lindsey Considered Favorites For Bulls GM Role</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285307/Matt-Lloyd-Bryson-Graham-Dennis-Lindsey-Considered-Favorites-For-Bulls-GM-Role</link>
      <description><p>The Chicago Bulls have reportedly completed their first round of interviews for a new head of basketball operations. Minnesota Timberwolves GM Matt Lloyd, Atlanta Hawks VP of basketball operations Bryson Graham and Detroit Pistons VP of basketball operations Dennis Lindsey are considered the finalists to be the Bulls next lead decision-maker.</p>
<p>Chicago has also met with Cleveland Cavaliers GM Mike Gansey and San Antonio Spurs assistant GM Dave Telep. All of the initial meetings were reportedly conducted virtually.</p>
<p>The one of the meetings with candidates took on a tone of a rebuild for Chicago. The Bulls fired Arturas Karnisovas from his role near the end of the season, and last week head coach Billy Donovan decided not to return for next season. That has the team primed for a full reset this offseason.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Sun, 26 Apr 2026 21:54:36 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285307/Matt-Lloyd-Bryson-Graham-Dennis-Lindsey-Considered-Favorites-For-Bulls-GM-Role</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/nba_chicagobulls.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>NBA Fines Nikola Jokic $50K, Julius Randle $35K For Game 4 Altercation</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285306/NBA-Fines-Nikola-Jokic-$50K-Julius-Randle-$35K-For-Game-4-Altercation</link>
      <description><p>The NBA has fined Denver Nuggets center Nikola Jokic $50,000 and Minnesota Timberwolves forward Julius Randle $35,000 for their roles in an on-court altercation in Saturday's playoff game.</p>
<p>With 1.3 seconds remaining in the game and the Wolves holding a big lead, Jokic initiated the confrontation by shoving Minnesota forward Jaden McDaniels. Randle escalated the situation by getting involved and shoving Nuggets guard Bruce Brown.</p>
<p>Both Jokic and Randle were ejected from the game. Despite it appearing that several players left their bench areas, no suspensions were handed out by the NBA.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Sun, 26 Apr 2026 21:41:45 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285306/NBA-Fines-Nikola-Jokic-$50K-Julius-Randle-$35K-For-Game-4-Altercation</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Jokic_Nikola_den_250131.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Joel Embiid Starting Game 4 For Sixers</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285305/Joel-Embiid-Starting-Game-4-For-Sixers</link>
      <description><p>Two weeks after an emergency appendectomy, Philadelphia 76ers center Joel Embiid is back in the lineup. Embiid is making his return to the lineup for Game 4 of the Sixers first-round playoff series against the Boston Celtics. Boston leads Philadelphia 2-1 in the series.</p>
<p>Embiid has dealt with various health issues throughout the season, but looked to be healthy heading into the postseason. Then, shortly before the Play-In Tournament, the All-Star center came down with what was thought to be an illness. Upon examination, it was found that Embiid had appendicitis, which necessitated surgery.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Sun, 26 Apr 2026 18:52:56 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285305/Joel-Embiid-Starting-Game-4-For-Sixers</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Embiid_Joel_phi_231130.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Pelicans Considering James Borrego, Several Long-Time Assistants For Next Head Coach</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285304/Pelicans-Considering-James-Borrego-Several-Long-Time-Assistants-For-Next-Head-Coach</link>
      <description><p>The New Orleans Pelicans are considering James Borrego to remain as their head coach, as well as looking at several long-time NBA assistant coaches. Borrego went 24-46 as the Pelicans interim head coach after he took over for Willie Green.</p>
<p>New Orleans is also considering multiple assistant coaches. San Antonio Spurs assistant Sean Sweeney, Milwaukee Bucks assistant Darvin Ham, and Brooklyn Nets assistant Steve Hetzel are all in the mix. The Pelicans are also hoping to interview Detroit Pistons assistant Jarrett Jack and Milwaukee Bucks assistant Rajon Rondo.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Sun, 26 Apr 2026 16:34:15 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285304/Pelicans-Considering-James-Borrego-Several-Long-Time-Assistants-For-Next-Head-Coach</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/nba_neworleanspelicans.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Sasha Vezenkov Named EuroLeague MVP</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285303/Sasha-Vezenkov-Named-EuroLeague-MVP</link>
      <description><p>Olympiacos forward Sasha Vezenkov has been named the 2025-26 EuroLeague MVP. This is the second time Vezenkov has been named EuroLeague MVP, after winning the award in for the 2022-23 campaign.</p>
<p>Vezenkov averaged a league-leading 19.4 point in EuroLeague play. He also averaged 6.6 rebounds, while leading Olympiacos to the top of the EuroLeague standings with a 26-12 record. </p></description>
      <pubDate>Sun, 26 Apr 2026 16:25:48 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285303/Sasha-Vezenkov-Named-EuroLeague-MVP</guid>

   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Kevin Durant Likely To Miss Game 4 Due To Bone Bruise In Sprained Left Ankle</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285302/Kevin-Durant-Likely-To-Miss-Game-4-Due-To-Bone-Bruise-In-Sprained-Left-Ankle</link>
      <description><p>The Houston Rockets are in the brink of elimination down 3-0 to the Los Angeles Lakers in their first-round playoff series. If Houston is going to hold off the end of their season, they're likely to have to do it without star forward Kevin Durant.</p>
<p>Durant is expected to be miss Game 4 due to a bone bruise in his sprained left ankle. The veteran forward has undergone around-the-clock treatment for his ankle, but hasn't seen enough improvement to try to play.</p>
<p>Houston has been without Durant for Games 1 and 3 of the series due to a knee injury for Game 1 and then the ankle injury, which Durant suffered during Game 2, kept Durant out for Game 3.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Sun, 26 Apr 2026 15:33:32 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285302/Kevin-Durant-Likely-To-Miss-Game-4-Due-To-Bone-Bruise-In-Sprained-Left-Ankle</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Durant_Kevin_hou_251010.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Donte DiVincenzo To Undergo Achilles Surgery On Sunday</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285301/Donte-DiVincenzo-To-Undergo-Achilles-Surgery-On-Sunday</link>
      <description><p>Minnesota Timberwolves guard Donte DiVincenzo is undergoing surgery for his ruptured Achilles' tendon on Sunday. DiVincenzo suffered the injury during the Wolves Game 4 victory over the Denver Nuggets on Saturday.</p>
<p>The veteran guard is having the surgery done at the Hospital for Special Surgery in New York. The surgery will be completed by Dr. Martin O'Malley. Dr. O'Malley completed Jayson Tatum's Achilles's repair at HSS in New York last year.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Sun, 26 Apr 2026 15:24:17 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285301/Donte-DiVincenzo-To-Undergo-Achilles-Surgery-On-Sunday</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/DiVincenzo_Donte_min_241015.jpg" />
   </item>
   <item>
      <title>Victor Wembanyama Clears Concussion Protocol, Available For Game 4</title>
      <link>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285300/Victor-Wembanyama-Clears-Concussion-Protocol-Available-For-Game-4</link>
      <description><p>San Antonio Spurs superstar Victor Wembanyama has cleared the NBA's concussion protocol. That means Wembanyama will be available to play in Sunday's Game 4 against the Portland Trail Blazers.</p>
<p>Wembanyama was hurt in the Spurs Game 1 victory over the Trail Blazers. He's missed the last two games, which the team's split. San Antonio leads Portland 2-1 and is looking to take a 3-1 series lead back to San Antonio on Tuesday.</p></description>
      <pubDate>Sun, 26 Apr 2026 15:19:05 EDT</pubDate>
      <guid>https://basketball.realgm.com/wiretap/285300/Victor-Wembanyama-Clears-Concussion-Protocol-Available-For-Game-4</guid>
      <media:thumbnail url="https://basketball.realgm.com/images/nba/4.2/wiretap/photos/2006/Wembanyama_Victor_sas_260226.jpg" />
   </item>
